title: hostname Switch1
cmd:
hostname Switch1                        # Set device hostname  
ip domain-name example.com              # Define domain name (needed for RSA key)  
crypto key generate rsa                 # Generate RSA keys for SSH  
username admin privilege 15 secret cisco123   # Create local admin user  
line vty 0 4                            # Enter VTY line configuration (remote access)  
 transport input ssh                    # Allow only SSH (disable Telnet)  
 login local                            # Use local user database for login
desc: SSH > Initial SSH Setup

title: ip ssh version 2
cmd:
ip ssh version 2                        # Enable SSH version 2 (more secure)  
ip ssh time-out 60                      # Set SSH idle timeout to 60 seconds  
ip ssh authentication-retries 3         # Allow max 3 login attempts
desc: SSH > Modifying SSH Config

title: show ip ssh
cmd:
show ip ssh
conf t
ip domain-name cisco.com
crypto key generate rsa
desc: Basic Networking > Setup > Configure SSH

title: username admin secret ccna
cmd:
username admin secret ccna
line vty 0 15
transport input ssh
login local
exit
ip ssh version 2
exit
desc: Basic Networking > Setup > Configure SSH

title: ip ssh time-out 60
cmd:
ip ssh time-out 60
ip ssh authentication-retries 3
end
desc: Basic Networking > Setup > Basic Hardening (Work Needed)

title: Host# conf t
cmd:
Host# conf t
Host(config)# username  <USER>  password <PASS>
Host(config)# show user-account
desc: SSH - How to login with SSH

title: Host(config)# aaa new-model
desc: SSH - How to login with SSH

title: Host(config)# ip domain-name example.com
cmd:
Host(config)# ip domain-name example.com
Host(config)# hostname test1
test1(config)# ip ssh version 2
test1(config)# crypto key generate rsa
How many bits in the modulus [512]:2048
desc: SSH - How to login with SSH

title: ssh  user01@192.168.0.5
desc: Configuration Flow > Configuration with set commands

title: ssh  user01@192.168.0.5
cmd:
ssh  user01@192.168.0.5
configure       <- configuration mode
load set terminal
(abbr)
Ctrl + D
show | compare   <- diff configuration
commit check     <- check configuration
commit           <- commit configuration. If you want to cancel this configuration, You can use  "rollback 0" command.
exit
exit
desc: Configuration Flow > Configuration with load set terminal

title: ssh  user01@192.168.0.5
cmd:
ssh  user01@192.168.0.5
configure 
edit XXXXXXX     <- edit is like cd of linux.
edit XXXXXXX
up
top
edit XXXXXX
show | display set
set XXXX
delete XXXX
show | compare
commit check
commit
exit             <- you need 'exit' command.
exit
desc: Configuration Flow > Step by Step Configuration with edit command

title: S1#show ip ssh
desc: Configuring SSH | Use it to verify that the switch supports SSH

title: S1(config-line)#exit
desc: Configuring SSH |

title: S1(config)#ip ssh version 2
desc: Configuring SSH | enable SSH version 2

title: S1(config)#crypto key zeroise rsa
desc: Configuring SSH | :warning: use to **delete** RSA key pair

title: S1(config)#ip ssh time-out [time]
desc: Configuring SSH > Modifying SSH configuration | Change timeout setting (time in seconds)

title: S1(config)#ip ssh authentication-retries [retries]
desc: Configuring SSH > Modifying SSH configuration | Change number of allowed authentication attempts

title: VTY access control
cmd:
line vty 0 4
 access-class MGMT in
 transport input ssh
 login local
desc: Restrict management plane access.

title: Security zones
cmd:
set security zones security-zone trust interfaces ge-0/0/1.0
set security zones security-zone untrust interfaces ge-0/0/0.0
set security zones security-zone trust host-inbound-traffic system-services [ ssh ping ]
desc: Map interfaces to zones.

title: ip ssh client source-interface
cmd:
ip ssh [client] source-interface INTERFACE [vrf vrf_name]
no ip ssh [client] source-interface
default ip ssh [client] source-interface
desc: The ip ssh client source-interface command specifies the source IP address for secure shell (SSH) connections.

title: management security entropy
cmd: management security entropy source [cpu jitter] [hardware exclusive]
desc: The management security entropy command manages the source of cryptographic entropy (randomness) and provides high quality randomness for SSH keys, SSL/TLS certificates, and random session identifiers.

title: management ssh
cmd:
management ssh
no management ssh
default management ssh
desc: The management ssh command places the switch in SSH Management Configuration Mode to adjust SSH session connection parameters.

title: timeout
desc: The timeout command in the SSH, Telnet, or Console Management Configuration Mode specifies the maximum length of a management session regardless of activity during the session.

title: logging console
cmd:
logging console [alerts | critical | debugging | emergencies | informational
no | default logging console [alerts | critical | debugging | emergencies |
informational | notifications | warnings]
desc: The logging console command in Global Configuration Mode controls which system log events display directly on the switch physical console port or on an administrator's remote terminal session such as SSH or

title: logging monitor
cmd:
logging monitor [alerts | critical | debugging | emergencies | informational
informational | notifications | warnings] 0_7
desc: The logging monitor command in Global Configuration Mode controls the display of system log events on a switch, specifically for remote terminal sessions such as SSH or Telnet.

title: AAA Commands
desc: Local Security File Commands • aaa root • enable password • show privilege • show users • show users accounts • username • username ssh-key Accounting, Authentication, and Authorization Commands • aaa accounting

title: username ssh-key
cmd:
username name sshkey KEY
no username name sshkey [role]
default username name sshkey [role]
desc: The username ssh-key command configures an SSH key for the specified username.

title: ip fhrp accept-mode
cmd:
ip fhrp accept-mode
no ip fhrp accept-mode
default ip fhrp accept-mode
desc: The ip fhrp accept-mode command configures the switch to permit SSH access to the VRRP Master and VARP Master router.
